questions.question: What is the primary capability that distinguishes Claude Code from Claude.ai?
options: Claude Code has direct access to your files, terminal, and codebase without requiring copy-paste
Claude Code can only be used in the terminal
Claude Code is free while Claude.ai requires a subscription
Claude Code can access the internet while Claude.ai cannot
questions.explanation: Claude Code's key differentiator is that it operates as an AI Agent with direct access to your files, terminal, and entire codebase, eliminating the need for manual copy-paste workflows. This direct integration is what sets it apart from Claude.ai, which requires manual code sharing.

questions.question: In which of the following environments can Claude Code be used?
options: Terminal, Visual Studio Code, Claude Desktop app, web, and JetBrains IDEs
Only in the terminal and Visual Studio Code
Only in the Claude Desktop app and on the web
Only in JetBrains IDEs and the terminal
questions.explanation: Claude Code is available across multiple platforms: your terminal, Visual Studio Code, the Claude Desktop app, on the web, and in JetBrains IDEs. This broad availability makes it accessible regardless of your preferred development environment.

questions.question: What is an AI Agent?
options: Software that can interact with its environment and perform actions to complete a defined goal
A tool that only reads code without making any changes
A service that requires human intervention for every single action
A program that can only work with text files
questions.explanation: An AI Agent is software designed to interact with its environment and perform actions autonomously to achieve a defined goal. It operates through a large language model in a loop and can access tools, external services, or other agents to accomplish its objectives.

questions.question: Which of the following actions can Claude Code perform on your codebase?
options: Read and understand code, edit files, run terminal commands, and search the web
Only read code and provide explanations
Only edit files without understanding the context
Only run terminal commands without reading files
questions.explanation: Claude Code can perform multiple integrated actions: read and understand your codebase, edit files across your project, run terminal commands, and search the web for documentation. This combination of capabilities enables it to work autonomously on complex tasks.

questions.question: What does Claude Code do before running commands or making changes to your files by default?
options: It asks for your permission first
It makes changes immediately without notification
It creates a backup and then proceeds automatically
It sends the changes to a review queue
questions.explanation: By default, Claude Code asks for permission before running commands or making changes. This design ensures you remain in control and can choose between a hands-on or hands-off approach, maintaining oversight of the tool's actions.

questions.question: How does Claude Code handle the limitation of its context window when working with large codebases?
options: It uses agentic strategies to locate answers within the codebase without loading everything into context at once
It requires you to manually select which files to include
It automatically deletes files that don't fit in the context window
It splits the codebase into separate projects
questions.explanation: Claude Code's agentic nature allows it to work strategically within its context window limitations. Rather than loading an entire codebase at once, it finds intelligent ways to locate and access the information it needs, making efficient use of its working memory.

questions.question: What is a potential limitation of Claude Code that users should be aware of?
options: It can make mistakes such as misunderstanding intent, introducing bugs, or over-engineering solutions
It cannot edit files in any circumstance
It always requires an internet connection to function
It can only work with Python code
questions.explanation: Like any tool, Claude Code is not perfect. It may misunderstand your intent, introduce bugs, or create overly complex solutions. Staying engaged in the process helps catch these errors early, which is why maintaining oversight is important.

questions.question: How does Claude Code's approach to refactoring differ from traditional manual refactoring?
options: Claude Code can refactor a function and automatically update all files that reference it in one operation
Claude Code can only refactor one file at a time
Claude Code requires you to manually update all references after refactoring
Claude Code cannot perform refactoring tasks
questions.explanation: Claude Code can refactor a function and update every file that references it across your project in a single operation. This capability demonstrates its advantage over manual refactoring, where developers must track down and update all references individually.

questions.question: What role does the loop mechanism play in how Claude Code operates as an AI Agent?
options: It allows the large language model to operate in real time, taking actions and responding to results iteratively
It prevents Claude Code from making any changes to files
It limits Claude Code to reading files only
It ensures that all operations happen simultaneously without waiting
questions.explanation: The core of an AI Agent is a large language model operating in a loop in real time. This loop mechanism enables Claude Code to take an action, observe the result, and then decide on the next step—creating an iterative, responsive workflow that adapts to changing conditions.

questions.question: How does Claude Code's ability to run terminal commands and use their output inform its subsequent actions?
options: It executes commands, analyzes the output, and uses that information to determine what to do next
It runs commands but ignores the output
It can only run commands that produce no output
It requires manual interpretation of all command outputs
questions.explanation: Claude Code can execute terminal commands (like build scripts or tests) and use the output to inform its next decisions. This feedback loop is central to its agentic behavior—it observes results and adapts its approach accordingly, enabling autonomous problem-solving.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains why staying in the loop with Claude Code is important for effective use?
options: Claude Code can make mistakes, and user oversight helps catch errors early before they compound
Claude Code cannot function without constant human direction for every action
The context window requires manual management by the user at all times
Claude Code needs permission for every single keystroke it makes
questions.explanation: While Claude Code operates autonomously as an agent, it is not infallible. Staying engaged allows you to catch misunderstandings, bugs, or over-engineered solutions early. This balance between automation and oversight is key to using the tool effectively and safely.

questions.question: How does Claude Code's access to external tools and services enhance its ability to function as an AI Agent?
options: It can leverage tools, external services, and other agents to reach its goals beyond what it could do alone
External tools replace the need for Claude Code to understand your codebase
Access to external services means Claude Code no longer needs permission from users
External tools eliminate the possibility of Claude Code making mistakes
questions.explanation: An AI Agent's power comes partly from its ability to access tools, external services, and other agents. Claude Code can search the web for documentation, integrate with your developer tools, and use other resources to accomplish goals more effectively than it could in isolation. This extensibility is a core strength of the agentic approach.
